projectDescription: A macOS Electron app that lets multiple players collaboratively share a keyboard to control any keyboard-driven game in real time over a local network.
keyFeatures: Real-time collaborative keyboard sharing over local network via Socket.IO
First-come, first-served key reservation — each player owns a unique subset of keys
macOS keyboard emulation via a native C helper using CoreGraphics CGEventPost
Browser-based player client — no install needed for players
Admin page accessible from any device on the network for full server control
CLI console commands for server management (stop, enable/disable emulation, waiting room, key revoke)
Configurable waiting room, key limits, rate limiting, and player name validation
Winston-based structured logging with file output and live admin page log stream
Electron desktop app with splash screen, display sleep prevention, and Accessibility permission flow
Key emulation supports letters, numbers, symbols, arrow keys, modifier keys, and F1–F20
publicApiSurface: Socket.IO event: setName(username) — set player name to begin playing
Socket.IO event: keydown(key) — send a key-down event to the host
Socket.IO event: keyup(key) — send a key-up event to the host
Socket.IO event (server→client): nameset — name accepted, gameplay unlocked
Socket.IO event (server→client): keyReserved(keyName) — player has been assigned a new key
Socket.IO event (server→client): log(content, format) — log message delivered to player UI
Socket.IO event (server→client): id(playerId) — unique player ID assigned on connect
Admin namespace (/admin) Socket.IO event: authenticate(password) — authenticate admin session
Admin namespace (/admin) Socket.IO event: command(text) — run a console command remotely
Admin namespace (server→client): authenticated — admin session confirmed
Admin namespace (server→client): response(command, output) — command result returned to admin
HTTP GET /admin — Admin control panel page
HTTP GET /keycodes — Plain-text table of all supported keys and their current state
HTTP GET /logs — Combined server log output
HTTP GET /logs/error — Error-only log output
HTTP GET /logs/warn — Warning-only log output
Console command: stop — terminate the server process
Console command: enable <emulation|reservation> — enable emulation or auto-reservation
Console command: disable <emulation|reservation> — disable emulation or auto-reservation
Console command: press <key> [press|down|up] — manually emulate a keypress
Console command: uri — print the server URL
Console command: list <all|active|wr|nameless> — list connected players
Console command: waitingroom <admit|dismiss> <id|all> — manage waiting room
Console command: key <revoke|enable|disable> <key|all> — manage key assignments
Console command: keycodes — print current keycode table
Console command: logs [combined|error|warn] — show log file location/URL
src/config.json — full configuration file for server, admin, logging, and player settings
